steps.title: Set up variables for total costs
steps.content:
Let $x$ = total cost of square tables and $y$ = total cost of hexagonal tables.

From the problem:
  • Total cost: $x + y = 28,000$
  • Revenues are $2x$ (square) and $2.5y$ (hexagonal), so total revenue: $2x + 2.5y = 67,200$

steps.title: Calculate the revenue difference
steps.content:
Revenue from hexagonal tables: $2.5y = 2.5(22,400) = 56,000$

Revenue from square tables: $2x = 2(5,600) = 11,200$

Revenue difference: $56,000 - 11,200 = 44,800$. That confirms D is correct.
